title: Hotel Check-in
description: Practice conversations for checking in, making requests, and resolving issues at a hotel

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Good evening and welcome to the Grand Meridian Hotel. Do you have a reservation with us?

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: Yes, I do. The reservation is under the name Thompson — James Thompson. I booked a Deluxe King room for three nights.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Let me pull that up for you, Mr. Thompson... Yes, I can see your reservation — a Deluxe King room with city view, checking in tonight and checking out on Saturday.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: That's correct. I'm hoping I might be able to get an upgrade if anything is available. I'm celebrating my wedding anniversary.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Congratulations on your anniversary! I'd be happy to check. We actually have a Junior Suite available tonight — it features a separate living area, a spa bath, and a panoramic city view. It's normally $120 more per night, but I can offer it to you at a complimentary upgrade as a gift from us.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: That's incredibly generous, thank you! My wife will love that.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Our pleasure. Could I have a credit card for incidentals, please? We'll place a hold of $200 which will be released upon checkout if there are no charges.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: Of course, here's my Visa. Also, could you please make sure our room is ready as soon as possible? We've just come off a long flight and we're exhausted.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Your suite is ready right now. Housekeeping prepared it just an hour ago. I'll let the bellhop know to take your bags up.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: That's wonderful. Could you also have some champagne and chocolates sent up? I pre-arranged a surprise for my wife before we arrived.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: I see the note in your reservation — the champagne and anniversary platter are already in the suite waiting for you.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: Perfect! Is the hotel spa open this late?

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: The spa is open until 10 PM tonight and from 7 AM tomorrow. Would you like me to book a couples' treatment for tomorrow morning?

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: Yes, please. Around 10 AM would be ideal.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: I've reserved the couples' massage for 10 AM tomorrow. Here are your key cards. Your suite is on the 18th floor — room 1804. The lift is just behind you. Breakfast is served from 6:30 to 10:30 AM in the Terrace Restaurant on the second floor. Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: What's the Wi-Fi password?

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: The password is on this card — GrandMeridian2024. It works throughout the hotel. And if you need anything at any time, just dial zero from your room phone.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: Thank you so much. You've made our anniversary very special already.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: That's our goal. Enjoy your stay, Mr. Thompson. Happy anniversary!

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: Excuse me, I need to speak with a manager. I have an issue with my room that hasn't been resolved.

lines.speaker: Manager
lines.text: Of course. I'm Lisa, the Duty Manager. I'm sorry to hear you're having a problem. What's the issue?

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: I'm in room 512. The air conditioning has not been working properly since I checked in two days ago. I reported it to the front desk yesterday morning and was told it would be fixed, but it still isn't working. My room is uncomfortably warm and I can't sleep.

lines.speaker: Manager
lines.text: I sincerely apologize for this, and especially for the delayed follow-through on your report. That is not acceptable. Let me check the maintenance log immediately.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: I appreciate that. I have one more night here and I really need a comfortable room to sleep in.

lines.speaker: Manager
lines.text: I completely understand. I can see the maintenance request was submitted but not yet completed — I'll escalate that right now. In the meantime, I'd like to move you to a comparable room on a higher floor where I know the climate control is working perfectly. Would that work for you?

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: That would be acceptable, yes. But I'd like to make sure everything is as described before I move all my belongings.

lines.speaker: Manager
lines.text: Absolutely. I'll personally take you to view room 718 before any decision is made. It's a Deluxe room on a corner with a park view — actually a slightly nicer view than your current room.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: I'd like to see it first, please.

lines.speaker: Manager
lines.text: Of course. Before we go up — as an apology for the inconvenience, I'd like to offer you dinner for two in our restaurant tonight, complimentary, and waive the resort fee for your stay.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: I appreciate that gesture. The inconvenience has been significant, though. Two nights without proper sleep has affected my work meetings today.

lines.speaker: Manager
lines.text: I understand completely. I'll also apply a 20% discount to your total room bill as additional compensation. I want to ensure you leave with a positive impression of us.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: That's fair. Thank you for taking this seriously. Let's go look at the room.

lines.speaker: Manager
lines.text: Right this way. And I'll make sure the porter assists with moving your luggage so it's as seamless as possible for you.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: I appreciate the personal attention. Room 718 looks lovely — much better than 512 honestly. I'll take it.

lines.speaker: Manager
lines.text: Wonderful. Your new keys are ready and I'll have your belongings transferred within 30 minutes. Once again, I'm truly sorry for the disruption to your stay.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: I appreciate how you handled this. I'll look forward to a good night's sleep finally.

lines.speaker: Room Service
lines.text: Good evening, room service. How may I help you?

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: Hi, I'd like to order dinner to my room, please. I'm in room 906.

lines.speaker: Room Service
lines.text: Of course, Ms. Anderson. What would you like to order?

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: Could I start with the French onion soup? And for the main course, I'll have the grilled chicken with roasted vegetables.

lines.speaker: Room Service
lines.text: Excellent choices. How would you like the chicken prepared — with our herb butter sauce or the lemon and thyme?

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: The lemon and thyme, please. Could I also get a side of mashed potatoes?

lines.speaker: Room Service
lines.text: Absolutely. Would you like anything to drink with your meal?

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: A glass of white wine, please. What do you recommend?

lines.speaker: Room Service
lines.text: We have a lovely Sauvignon Blanc from New Zealand, or a Chardonnay from California. Both pair well with chicken.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: I'll go with the Sauvignon Blanc. And could I order dessert now or should I call back later?

lines.speaker: Room Service
lines.text: You can order now if you'd like. We'll bring dessert separately about 30 minutes after your main course.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: In that case, I'll have the chocolate lava cake. How long will the order take?

lines.speaker: Room Service
lines.text: Your meal should be delivered within 35 to 40 minutes. Shall I set up the dining tray by the window or the desk area?

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: By the window would be lovely. One more thing — I have an early morning flight tomorrow. Is it possible to order a breakfast box for a 5 AM departure?

lines.speaker: Room Service
lines.text: Certainly. We offer an early departure breakfast that includes a croissant, fresh fruit, yogurt, orange juice, and coffee in a takeaway bag. I can have it delivered to your room at 4:30 AM or left at the front desk.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: Delivered to the room at 4:30 would be perfect. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.text: Good morning. I'm visiting the city for the first time and I was hoping you could recommend some things to see and do.

lines.speaker: Concierge
lines.text: Welcome! I'd be happy to help. How many days do you have, and what are your interests?

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.text: I'm here for three days. I love history, local food, and art museums. I'm not so interested in touristy shopping districts.

lines.speaker: Concierge
lines.text: You're in for a treat. For history, I'd strongly recommend the Old Town walking tour. It's a two-hour guided walk through the medieval quarter. Tours depart twice daily at 10 AM and 2 PM.

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.text: That sounds wonderful. How do I book it?

lines.speaker: Concierge
lines.text: I can book it for you right now. It's $25 per person. Would you prefer the morning or afternoon tour?

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.text: The morning tour, please. What about restaurants? I'd love to try authentic local cuisine, not tourist traps.

lines.speaker: Concierge
lines.text: For authentic local food, I recommend three places. First, Casa Manuel in the old quarter — it's family-run and serves traditional dishes that locals love. Second, the Central Market has incredible street food stalls. Third, for a special evening meal, La Terraza has a rooftop with stunning views and a seasonal menu.

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.text: Do I need reservations for La Terraza?

lines.speaker: Concierge
lines.text: I'd strongly recommend it, especially for dinner. I can make a reservation for you. What evening and how many guests?

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.text: Tomorrow evening, just for one. Is that awkward, dining alone?

lines.speaker: Concierge
lines.text: Not at all! Many guests dine solo and it's completely normal. I'll request a nice table with a view for you. Eight o'clock?

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.text: Perfect. And for art — which museum would you prioritize if I only have time for one?

lines.speaker: Concierge
lines.text: The National Gallery is the must-see. They have an incredible collection spanning five centuries. Allow at least three hours. Admission is free but I'd suggest going early to avoid crowds.

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.text: What's the best way to get around the city? Should I use taxis or public transport?

lines.speaker: Concierge
lines.text: The metro is excellent — fast, clean, and very affordable. I can give you a transit card with unlimited rides for three days. It's only $15 and covers buses and the metro.

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.text: That's a great deal. I'll take one. Thank you so much — you've planned my entire trip!

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: Good morning. I'd like to check out, please. Room 1204.

lines.speaker: Front Desk
lines.text: Good morning, Mr. Patel. I hope you enjoyed your stay. Let me pull up your bill.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: Yes, it was an excellent stay overall. The room was very comfortable.

lines.speaker: Front Desk
lines.text: I'm glad to hear that. Here's your itemized bill. The room charges for four nights come to $920, plus the restaurant charge of $85 from Tuesday evening, and the spa treatment for $120. The total is $1,125.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: Could I review the bill? I don't recall a restaurant charge of $85.

lines.speaker: Front Desk
lines.text: Let me check the details. It shows a charge at the Terrace Restaurant on Tuesday at 8:15 PM. Does that ring a bell?

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: Oh yes, you're right. I forgot about the wine. That's correct then.

lines.speaker: Front Desk
lines.text: Shall I charge the total to the Visa card on file?

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: Yes, please. Could I also get a receipt emailed to me? I'll need it for my expense report.

lines.speaker: Front Desk
lines.text: Of course. I'll send the detailed receipt to the email address we have on file. Is it still david.patel@company.com?

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: That's correct. One more thing — I have a late flight this evening. Would it be possible to leave my luggage here until this afternoon?

lines.speaker: Front Desk
lines.text: Absolutely. Our luggage storage is complimentary for the day of checkout. Just bring your bags to the bell desk and they'll give you a claim ticket.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: That's very convenient. And could you arrange a taxi to the airport for around 5 PM?

lines.speaker: Front Desk
lines.text: I'll schedule that now. The taxi will be waiting at the main entrance at 5 PM. The ride to the airport takes about 40 minutes, depending on traffic.

lines.speaker: Guest
lines.text: Perfect. Thank you for everything. I'll definitely be coming back on my next business trip.

lines.speaker: Front Desk
lines.text: We'd love to have you back, Mr. Patel. As a returning guest, you'll automatically receive a 10% discount on your next booking. Safe travels!
